back: An entity set is a collection (or set) of entities of the same type that share the same attributes. For example, a 'Student' entity set contains all student entities.

front: What are the two main types of structural constraints on relationships?
back: The two main types are Cardinality Ratio and Participation Constraints. Cardinality ratio specifies the maximum number of relationship instances an entity can participate in. Participation constraint specifies whether the existence of an entity depends on its being related to another entity (total vs. partial participation).

front: Define the cardinality ratios for a binary relationship.
back: The cardinality ratios for a binary relationship are: One-to-One (1:1), One-to-Many (1:N), Many-to-One (N:1), and Many-to-Many (M:N). They describe the maximum number of entities from one set that can be associated with a single entity from the other set.

front: What is the difference between total and partial participation?
back: Total participation (existence dependency) means every entity in an entity set must participate in the relationship. It is represented by a double line in an ER diagram. Partial participation means that not every entity is required to participate; it is represented by a single line.

front: How are a weak entity set and its identifying relationship related to structural constraints?
back: A weak entity set is existence-dependent on a strong entity set. Its participation in the identifying relationship is always total (represented by a double line). Furthermore, the relationship is often one-to-many from the strong to the weak entity set.
